title: [CI:BUILD] Cirrus: Fix building multiarch images
body:
The caching update made in
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14016 neglected to attend to
the "git repo." needs of this task.  Fix this so images can build.

Also, make this optional task only appear when the `[CI:BUILD]` magic
string is used.  No reason to tempt every PR author to press a
mysterious button labeled 'trigger'.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
@TomSweeneyRedHat this should fix the problem you e-mailed me about.  Turns out as no surprise to me at all: it was my fault :grin: 

I will pre-trigger the test-build tasks (`Build multi-arch...`) in CI for this PR, to confirm the fix...
One question which is probably my misunderstanding.  Tests still are not completely happy.
@cevich I see that tests are green now (must've been a flake). Is this ready for review?

No response. I'll just assume it's reviewable.

New task map: ![cirrus-map-pr14240](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/168890836-ad3b89bb-cebf-41ce-a309-d8aff9fa1b32.png)
...basically, `ext_svc_check` has moved up to a lonely little corner at top left; `image_build` and `image_build_test` now depend on `build` instead. That part LGTM. So does the `CI:BUILD` part. The `main_script` change ... that's beyond my knowledge of cirrus yaml, but CI is green, so I'm going to assume it works.

/lgtm
/hold

Removing LGTM due to one concern: you have a comment under `test_image_build_task` that reads:
```
    # This takes a LONG time, only run when requested.  N/B: Any task
     # made to depend on this one will block FOREVER unless triggered.
```
However, the task map diagram distinctly shows an arrow from `test_image_build` to `success`. That kept bothering me, enough to make me poke at it again. I finally fixed (removed) that arrow by:
```diff
diff --git a/.cirrus.yml b/.cirrus.yml
index 62adace32..f7b51d904 100644
--- a/.cirrus.yml
+++ b/.cirrus.yml
@@ -724,6 +724,7 @@ image_build_task: &image-build                                                                                                             
 
 test_image_build_task:
     <<: *image-build
+    alias: test_image_build
     # Allow this to run inside a PR w/ [CI:BUILD]
     only_if: $CIRRUS_PR != '' && $CIRRUS_CHANGE_TITLE =~ '.*CI:BUILD.*'
     # This takes a LONG time, only run when requested.  N/B: Any task
```
...and I'm wondering if you might not want to include that, just to make sure we don't run into that block-forever problem you mention.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
New task-map with the arrow gone. I like this a lot better. ![cirrus-map-pr14240](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/265639/168895184-36280d34-8197-40fd-a638-908e1114c1af.png)
Still no response. Adding WIP label to prevent this from getting merged unintentionally.

Sorry, I was on PTO last week.

> ...and I'm wondering if you might not want to include that, just to make sure we don't run into that block-forever problem you mention.

Good catch, yeah this is a special case due to the 'only_if" constraint - task won't be created unless satisfied, thus the 'success' dependency would be ignored, otherwise it will block for `[CI:BUILD]` which is less ideal and certainly not developer-friendly.  I'll poke at this, rebase and force-push...

> map diagram distinctly shows an arrow from test_image_build to success

For reference, this is because it was (incorrectly) re-using the alias `image_build` - thus replacing the entire task (from Cirrus-CI's POV).  Hence the arrow, since `image_build` is a dependency for success (but only runs under the `multiarch` cron job).  This is why properly overriding the `alias` value fixes the problem.
I triggered the 'hello' and 'stable' test image builds because they're the fastest ones.  I'm assuming the others would pass, since they did before.

>this is because it was (incorrectly) re-using the alias image_build

From a YAML point of view, it seems correct. It's just from our POV that it's unintended/undesired :)

/lgtm
